Code changes:
1) Added `SwarmWorkQueue::config(&self) -> &WorkQueueConfig` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_work_queue.rs` to restore intended read-only config access from scheduler code.
2) Updated Rust 2024-incompatible reference-pattern closures in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_scheduler.rs` (`check_rebalance`) to explicit tuple-field comparisons.
3) Fixed borrow conflict in `SwarmScheduler::evaluate` by removing the immutable `ready_items()` borrow and gating assignment phase via `stats.ready > 0`, allowing safe mutable `queue.pull(...)` calls.

Validation (offloaded via rch):
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm --tests` (with isolated target dir)
- Remote workers repeatedly failed (SSH/rsync feature mismatch), rch fail-opened local.
- Result: prior scheduler errors (`queue.config()` missing, reference-pattern errors, E0502 in evaluate) are gone.
- Current failure is now an unrelated pre-existing parser mismatch in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/export.rs` (unclosed/mismatched delimiter around tests near lines ~534/3092).

Conclusion: this bead slice successfully cleared scheduler-specific compile blockers and exposed the next external blocker upstream.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:24:54Z"}]}
